title: AppModel
description: The single source of truth that the reasoning layer produces and the generator consumes. This schema is the CONTRACT between the (non-deterministic) LLM stage and the (deterministic) code-generation stage. The LLM's only job is to fill this object correctly; it never writes code. If the output validates against this schema, the generator can always produce a working harness. Everything downstream — React components, data files, data-testids, the manifest, the verifier checks — is derived mechanically from this document.

meta.description: Provenance and identity of the mock. Lets us trace any output back to the source crawl and the model/prompt that produced it (important for reproducibility and debugging).

appName.description: Human-friendly name inferred for the app, e.g. 'Instamart'. Used in titles and the package name.

appType.description: Coarse category inferred from the crawl. Drives NOTHING in the generator logic (the generator is generic); it exists only for reporting and for the verifier's faithfulness heuristics.

modelVersion.description: Identifier of the LLM + prompt version used, e.g. 'meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct/reasoning-v3'. Pinned for reproducibility.

crawlEvidenceHash.description: SHA of the crawl evidence bundle this model was derived from. Same hash => cached model can be reused (determinism + cost control).

designTokens.description: Extracted, NOT invented. Computed styles harvested from the live app during the crawl, normalized into a small theme. The generator emits these into tailwind.config.js so regenerated components match the source app's look without copying its markup.

colors.description: Semantic color roles mapped to hex values observed in the source. Keep the palette small; collapse near-duplicates.

fontFamily.description: Primary font stack observed, e.g. "'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif".

scale.description: Observed type scale, smallest to largest.

spacing.description: Spacing scale observed (margins/paddings/gaps). Keys are scale steps, values are px/rem.

entities.description: The app's DATA MODEL, inferred from API payloads where available and synthesized to match the observed UI otherwise. These become typed local-data files (e.g. products.js). This is what makes the harness backend-free: all data is local and deterministic.

components.description: Reusable UI components identified across screens (the repeated, role-bearing building blocks — ProductCard, CategoryPill, CartLineItem). De-duplicating these is what keeps generated code clean and DRY instead of one giant blob per screen.

screens.description: The distinct screens of the app. Each screen is a route. A screen owns a set of variants (the happy path PLUS edge states like empty/error/loading) — edge-state completeness is a first-class requirement, not an afterthought.

flows.description: Named user journeys expressed as ordered paths through screens via interactions. This is the graph an AI agent walks. Every flow must be fully connected end-to-end (the verifier asserts no dead ends).

identifier.description: camelCase identifier safe to use as a JS variable / React prop name.

kebabId.description: kebab-case id safe to use in routes and data-testids.

refEntity.description: Required when type is 'ref' or 'array' of refs: the entity this points to.

seed.description: Concrete seed records used as the local dataset. Must be deterministic (no random generation at runtime). Enough rows to make the UI look real (e.g. 8-20 products).

role.description: What this component is, semantically, e.g. 'displays a single product with image, title, price, and add-to-cart button'.

boundToEntity.description: If this component renders an entity, which one.

uiHint.description: Semantic layout priority: 'title' = primary headline, 'metadata' = secondary info (points/domain/age/author), 'content' = body text, 'hidden' = internal ids. Drives the generated visual hierarchy without a vision model.

interactiveElements.description: Actionable elements inside this component. Each gets a stable data-testid so agents can address it. The {id} placeholder is interpolated with the bound entity's id at render time, giving per-instance addressability (e.g. add-to-cart-btn-{id}).

testId.description: Stable, semantic data-testid. MUST be derived from role, not from source CSS classes. May contain a single {id} placeholder for per-instance elements.

label.description: Accessible label / visible text. Also used as aria-label when no text content.

action.description: What happens on interaction. Either a navigation, a local state mutation, or both. Kept declarative so the generator wires it deterministically and the verifier can predict outcomes.

targetScreen.description: For navigate: the screen id to go to.

mutation.description: For mutateState: a declarative description of the local-state change, e.g. add item to cart context.

payloadFrom.description: Where the payload comes from, e.g. 'boundEntity' or a field name.

purpose.description: One sentence on what the user does here. Helps the LLM reason about which edge states are needed.

layout.description: Coarse layout composition: which components appear and in what region. Deliberately coarse — pixel-perfection is not the goal; recognizable structure is.

components.description: Component names placed in this region.

repeats.description: True if the component(s) render once per entity record (e.g. a grid of ProductCards).

repeatsOver.description: Entity to iterate over when repeats is true.

variants.description: The states this screen can be in. 'default' is the happy path. Edge variants (empty/error/loading/unavailable) are REQUIRED where they make sense for the screen's purpose — this is the edge-state-completeness property, enforced.

description.description: What the user sees in this state and why it occurs.

trigger.description: How an agent reaches this variant, e.g. 'cart has zero items' or '?state=empty query param'. The generator exposes a deterministic switch (query param) so agents can force any variant.

steps.description: Ordered steps. Each step is an interaction performed on a screen that lands on the next screen. The verifier executes these in order against the built harness to prove the flow is navigable and deterministic.

screen.description: Screen the agent is on for this step.

variant.description: Optional: the variant to start in (defaults to 'default').

testId.description: The data-testid of the element to act on. The {id} placeholder may be resolved to a concrete seed id.

expectScreen.description: Screen the agent should land on after the action. Asserted by the verifier.
